hints: No two same-gender adjacent forces strict alternation.
Two patterns: BGBG… or GBGB…; each has 5!·5! arrangements.
solution: With $5$ boys and $5$ girls, no two of the same gender adjacent means the row must alternate, giving $2$ gender patterns. Each fills with $5!\cdot5!$ orders, so $2\cdot120\cdot120=28800.$

hints: Radial density on the unit disk is f(r)=2r.
solution: For a uniform point on the unit disk the radius has density $f(r)=2r$, so $\mathbb{E}[R]=\int_0^1 2r^2\,dr=\tfrac23.$

hints: Segments are disjoint iff one pair is exactly the two smallest points.
The lowest point pairs with the 2nd-lowest with probability 1/3.
solution: The two segments fail to overlap only when one segment's endpoints are the two smallest of the four points, i.e. the lowest point is paired with the second-lowest — probability $\tfrac13$. So overlap occurs with probability $1-\tfrac13=\tfrac23.$

hints: As the writer you keep the $2.50 premium; you lose once S exceeds K.
Breakeven when payoff S−K equals the premium.
solution: The writer keeps the $\$2.50$ premium and starts losing when $S-K$ exceeds it, so breakeven is $K+\text{premium}=12+2.50=\$14.50.$

hints: A maximal black run of length L contributes ⌊L/5⌋ non-overlapping components.
Sum the expected contributions over all runs (or count 5-windows minus the overlap correction).
The exact expectation is 284785/1048576, so p+q=1333361.
solution: Each maximal black run of length $L$ yields $\lfloor L/5\rfloor$ non-overlapping $5$-components. Summing the expected contribution across all runs of the $20$-cell line gives $\mathbb{E}=\dfrac{284785}{1048576}$, so $p+q=1333361.$

hints: The sides are degenerate — no actual triangle (zero area).
solution: Since $55+62=117$, the 'triangle' is degenerate with zero area, so the cost is $\$0.$

hints: Subtract 1 from each die: yᵢ≥0 with Σyᵢ=4 (upper bound 5 never binds since 4<5).
solution: Writing each die as $1+y_i$ with $y_i\ge0$, we need $\sum y_i=4$ (the cap of $5$ never binds). The count is $\binom{4+7}{7}=\binom{11}{7}=330$, so $x=330.$

hints: The mean absolute difference of two U(0,1) is 1/3.
Let Iᵢ indicate face i is red; E[T]=6·E[I₁] by symmetry.
solution: By symmetry $\mathbb{E}[T]=6\,P(\text{a given face red})$. Each face of the big cube paints $10^2$ of the $10^3$ small cubes, so $P=\tfrac1{10}$ and $\mathbb{E}[T]=\tfrac6{10}=\tfrac35.$
With $K=0$, the straddle is worth $v=\mathbb{E}[|S|]=\sqrt{2/\pi}$ for $S\sim N(0,1)$. Then $v^2=\dfrac2\pi$, so $a=2.$

hints: The heavy bean is known to be heavier, so each weighing has 3 outcomes locating it among thirds.
solution: Since the odd bean is known to be heavier, each weighing's three outcomes narrow it to a third. As $3^2=9<18\le27=3^3$, the minimum is $3$ weighings.

Conditioning on Jimmy's number $j$, $P(\text{Simon}>j)=\tfrac{3000-j}{3000}$. Averaging over $j=1..1000$: $\dfrac{1}{1000\cdot3000}\sum_{j=1}^{1000}(3000-j)=\dfrac{2499500}{3000000}=\dfrac{4999}{6000}.$
